analysis.root_cause: generateSectionHTML is a single, monolithic function that handles many unrelated section types (Profile, Experience, Education, Skills, Languages, Projects, Publications, Awards, References) with extensive inline data extraction, formatting, logging, and HTML templating logic all in one switch statement.
analysis.explanation: The method is complex and large because it mixes multiple responsibilities: (1) selecting behavior via a large switch on section.name, (2) transforming heterogeneous data models for each section into HTML, (3) performing formatting (e.g., date formatting and conditional “Present/Ongoing” logic), and (4) emitting debug logs for each case. Each case repeats similar patterns (checking array length, mapping items to HTML strings, building common wrapper markup like <div class="section"> and <div class="item">, and using fallback strings). As more section types are added, the switch grows further, increasing cyclomatic complexity and making the function harder to read, test, and modify without risking regressions. This violates SRP and Separation of Concerns by bundling unrelated concerns into one function, DRY by duplicating mapping/wrapper/conditional rendering patterns across cases, and KISS by requiring developers to understand many branches and formatting rules in a single place.

refactor.refactoring_technique: Extract Method with helper dispatch table
refactor.reasoning: This refactor reduces the large/complex method issue more aggressively by moving each section’s rendering logic into dedicated helper functions and replacing the switch body with a small dispatch table. It preserves the same section names, fallbacks, logging, empty-list behavior, and HTML structure while making generateSectionHTML much smaller and easier to read and maintain.

validation.comments: The refactor significantly reduces the original complex/large method by extracting each section into dedicated functions and using a dispatch table (sectionRenderers). This lowers cyclomatic complexity in generateSectionHTML and improves readability/maintainability. Behavior appears preserved: each renderer uses the same sectionData fallbacks and returns the same HTML/empty string logic; generateSectionHTML still logs section info and returns '' for unknown sections. No new functional differences or obvious edge-case changes were introduced.

selection.policy: best iteration: preserves_behavior (yes > uncertain > no), then higher improvement_score, then lower attempt number
